free_text: Angular distributions of deuterons from (p,d) reactions
in light nuclei.
II. Lithium, beryllium, boron, fluorine, and aluminum.
Description of experiment is given.
A thin NaI crystal (~0.01 in.) in a
scintillation counter was used.
Level energy value was added in Sub 005,009
and 011. REACTION code in Sub 005, 009, 011 was
changed.
A foil of lithium having a thickness of 1.3 mg/cm2 was
used as a target.
Angular digitizing error
Data digitizing error
Error from the graphic bars are compounded of
two parts. These are the standard deviation of the
total number of deuteron counts taken at the particular
angle and an estimated uncertainty in subtracting
the proton background from the deuteron spectrum.
Fig.2,3
A foil of lithium having a thickness of 1.3 mg/cm2 was
used as a target.
Error in cross section definition
